oneLiner: Light, refreshing protein that fits your everyday healthy lifestyle—wellness without sacrifice.
positioningStatement: PEP helps people make healthier everyday choices with a light, refreshing protein drink that feels like a treat—never a chore. Designed as a lifestyle accessory for wellness-focused, identity-driven people who want health without effort. PEP stands out for its minimal, pastel aesthetic and a taste experience worthy of the best moments of your day: café brunches, sunny co-working sessions, and afternoon refreshment.
audience: People upgrading from Kombucha, OLIPOP, or sparkling iced tea
Consumers seeking non-sugary, not-overly functional drinks that feel like a lifestyle choice, not a supplement
occasions: Brunch at a café with friends

voice.tone: Upbeat, light, warm, welcoming, subtly premium; modern and unfussy—never gym-focused or clinical.

visual.mood: Minimal, pastel, elegant with a soft, natural palette. Clean but lively—a gentle blend of fresh and sophisticated.
visual.photography: Bright, airy, real-life moments around cafés, coworking spaces, beaches, and relaxed lifestyle settings. People look healthy, social, and stylish but never posed or fitness-driven.
visual.typography: Blend of sophisticated serif (Didot), friendly sans-serif (Montserrat), and playful script (Brittany Signature)—as detailed in brand.json.
rejectLooks: Energy drink visuals (neon, metallic, high contrast, 'extreme' effects)

inLineBrands.name: BLOOM
inLineBrands.why: Integrates prebiotic benefits with fun, lively lifestyle branding, appealing to health-conscious consumers seeking trendy, day-to-day wellness.

inLineBrands.name: EVER TREE
inLineBrands.why: Minimalist, elegant design and natural ingredient focus encourage healthy choices without feeling clinical. Resonates with café and brunch occasions.

inLineBrands.name: OLIPOP
inLineBrands.why: Soft, approachable lifestyle branding with health-first messaging. Appeals to wellness-focused, identity-driven consumers.

inLineBrands.name: POPP
inLineBrands.why: Refreshing, lively wellness drink designed for everyday moments—matches PEP's ambitions for café and coworking occasions.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: HOLY
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Targets functional vitamin-need states with vitamin-forward messaging and clinical visuals — lacks PEP's lifestyle/aspirational, treat-like appeal.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: LMNT
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Focused on functional hydration & electrolytes; branding is utilitarian, functional, and not lifestyle or wellness aspirational.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: NOCCO
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Athlete-optimized BCAA/energy product; gym- and performance-centric, incompatible with PEP’s relaxed, lifestyle-forward context.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: PROTOCHOL
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Protein plus alcohol fusion aimed at the social/experimental segment; neither lifestyle nor pure wellness, and risks confusing PEP's 'everyday treat' focus.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: VITAMIN WELL
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Functional hydration and vitamin focus, more conventional and less emotionally resonant than PEP's 'fits my lifestyle' proposition.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Kin Euphorics
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Purpose-built social mood drink, designed for gatherings and experience, with high-concept, party-centric branding. Not focused on day-to-day lifestyle enhancement.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Lyre's
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Alcohol replacement for classic cocktail occasions—sophisticated but clearly social; not everyday or lifestyle upgrade.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Seedlip
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Premium non-alcoholic spirit for the social ritual, not habitual wellness. High-end party/host occasions rather than daily café or cowork.

antiReferences.name: Lean Body / gym RTD
antiReferences.why: Strongly ‘gym-bro’, bulk/protein-first, and masculine/functional. Violates lifestyle and approachable emotional fit for PEP.

antiReferences.name: drinkpep.com
antiReferences.why: Different product (energy/probiotic), name collision, branding and messaging incompatible with premium, pastel, lifestyle/social context.

antiReferences.name: Anything with sports/energy positioning
antiReferences.why: Confuses the approachable, aspirational lifestyle upgrade with functional or performance claims.

brandFitRules: Reject overtly functional, bold, gym-oriented visuals, words, or headlines.
Only approve brands with soft, pastel palettes and minimal, elegant layouts.
Photography must portray aspirational but achievable everyday moments (no staged fitness or party scenes).
Brand tone should always be light, joyful, and subtly premium—not intense, performative, or hyper-energetic.
No clinical, pharmaceutical, or performance-first copy or iconography.
Any website or packaging must reflect clarity, negative space, and warm minimalism.
Never use: neon, metallic, or ‘extreme’ color combos; ‘ripped’, ‘max out’, or similar words.
